role_title: Product Manager, LATAM (Miami or San Francisco)

location.office_time_required: occasional work at an Airbnb office or attendance at offsites, as agreed to with your manager

level_signals: Demonstrated track record of product leadership
Comp range $224K-$280K base, consistent with Senior IC / Principal PM at Airbnb
Reports to Product Marketing Director for International Markets
Comfortable with doing individual contributor product management work

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Demonstrated track record of product leadership.
Comfortable with doing individual contributor product management work.
Success in building and scaling global consumer products and ecosystems that people love.
Experience creating product messaging and delivering to customers and the media.
Ability to effectively use data and perform business analysis to support product strategy/decisions.
Capable of driving execution across teams in a fast-paced environment.
Ability and willingness to travel frequently (30-35% of the time).
Fluency in English and at least one other local language (Spanish or Portuguese).
the successful applicant will need to be based in San Francisco or Miami to be able to conduct their work.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Entrepreneurial track record of taking an idea to reality – start up experience a plus while capable of operating in a larger organization.

explicitly_excluded: Candidates located in Alaska, Indiana, Nebraska, North Dakota, Ohio, South Dakota, Wisconsin, Alabama, Mississippi, Oklahoma, Delaware, and Rhode Island

scope.surface: LATAM regional product management and product marketing for Airbnb consumer products
primary_metrics_inferred: Product feature adoption and rollout in LATAM markets
Clearly measurable OKRs for regional product initiatives
New and expanded business partnerships in LATAM
core_responsibilities: Build deep expertise on Airbnb in your market/region through user research, marketplace data, and competitive landscape analysis
Work with functional leaders to articulate product vision, strategy, and roadmap
Implement best-in-class product development and management practices including go-to-market kits
Maintain and articulate a product feature and roadmap prioritization plan
Advocate for and execute new products that deliver impact
Communicate and influence outcomes across all levels including executive teams
Drive alignment across product and cross-functional teams
Define goals and success with clearly measurable OKRs
Build and integrate partnerships in LATAM
Navigate regulatory and government requirements in partnership with Policy/Comms
Craft product narrative and marketing strategies for internal and external audiences
Develop and deliver product demos to press

compensation.equity_form: eligible for bonus, equity, benefits, and Employee Travel Credits

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly disclosed and competitive ($224K-$280K base)
Clear geographic scope and product surface (LATAM regional PM)
Role has explicit language fluency requirement signaling genuine regional focus
Equity and bonus mentioned in addition to base
Encourages diverse applicants and explicitly mentions disability accommodation
red_flags: Role title says 'Product Manager' but JD body repeatedly references 'Product Marketing' team and functions — unclear if this is a PM or PMM role; creates ambiguity
30-35% travel requirement is substantial and non-negotiable
Physical presence in San Francisco or Miami strictly required, eliminating fully-remote candidates
Long list of excluded US states for remote work may complicate future flexibility
Job title, team name ('Product Marketing'), and responsibilities mix product management and product marketing duties without clean delineation

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range + years range)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not directly stated)
ambiguities: The role title is 'Product Manager' but the team is called 'Product Marketing' and many responsibilities are product marketing in nature (messaging, press demos, go-to-market kits, comms). It is unclear whether this is a PM or PMM role. Archetype set to Generalist PM with secondary_archetype noting the PMM flavor.
Work auth / visa sponsorship: JD does not explicitly state whether sponsorship is offered or not. Marked unclear.

Location type: The JD requires physical presence in SF or Miami but also references occasional office attendance 'as agreed to with your manager,' suggesting some hybrid flexibility rather than full on-site. Treated as hybrid.
